panels.description: The percentage of all frontend requests that are not internal errors.
panels.title: Request Success Rate

panels.description: Total frontend requests received in the time range.
panels.title: Total Requests

panels.description: The total sum over the time range of input tokens processed by the frontend.
panels.title: Input Tokens

panels.description: The total sum over the time range of output tokens processed by the frontend.
panels.title: Output Tokens

panels.description: The average time to first token in ms over the time range.
panels.title: Average TTFT

panels.description: The average inter token latency over the time range.
panels.title: Average ITL

panels.description: The average e2e latency in seconds of requests processed by the frontend.
panels.title: Average E2E Latency
Frontend RPS
panels.description: Frontend request throughput in requests per second, scoped to the selected model.

panels.title: E2E Request Latency (p50 / p90 / p99)
panels.description: End-to-end frontend request latency percentiles. Measures total elapsed time from request arrival to response completion.
Frontend request rate broken down by terminal outcome. Success is shown separately from error types.
panels.title: Request Outcome Breakdown

panels.description: Current frontend request concurrency. Queued requests cover the period before first token; inflight requests cover the full request lifetime.
panels.title: Inflight vs Queued Requests

panels.description: Frontend time to first token percentiles. Measures latency from request start until the first token is emitted.
panels.title: TTFT (p50 / p90 / p99)

panels.description: Frontend inter-token latency percentiles. Measures decode-time latency between emitted tokens.
panels.title: ITL (p50 / p90 / p99)

panels.description: Frontend input sequence length distribution. Shows prompt-size percentiles and average input tokens per request.
panels.title: ISL Distribution

panels.description: Frontend output sequence length distribution. Shows completion-size percentiles and average output tokens per request.
panels.title: Output Size Distribution

panels.description: Frontend cached prompt tokens per request. Shows prefix-cache hit distribution from normalized backend usage accounting.
panels.title: Cached Tokens

panels.description: Per-worker active decode KV blocks over time, derived from frontend KV-router bookkeeping rather than direct worker-reported frontend request metrics.
panels.title: Per-Worker Active Decode Blocks

panels.description: Per-worker active prefill tokens over time, derived from frontend KV-router bookkeeping rather than direct worker-reported frontend request metrics. Meaningful in KV routing mode.
panels.title: Per-Worker Active Prefill Tokens

panels.description: Predicted KV cache hit rate at routing time. Calculated by the KV router as overlap_blocks / input_sequence_blocks for the selected worker, then recorded as a histogram.
panels.title: KV Hit Rate Distribution

panels.description: KV-router routing overhead breakdown by phase. Shows average, p50, and p90 for block hashing, indexer matching, sequence hashing, scheduling, and total routing overhead.
panels.title: Routing Overhead Breakdown (avg / p50 / p90)

panels.description: KV cache events applied to the index, broken down by event type and apply status. Similar to request outcome breakdown, this shows both healthy index updates and error cases over time.
panels.title: KV Events Applied Breakdown

panels.description: Worker-side request breakdown over time, broken out by instance. Shows total request rate, cancellation rate, and error rate by error_type from dynamo_component metrics. Note: errors_total is diagnostic rather than a clean mutually-exclusive failed-request counter; publish_response errors can include cancellation-related cases.
panels.title: Worker Request Breakdown Per Worker

panels.description: Worker-side request duration percentiles over time, broken down per worker by instance. This measures total time spent processing requests inside the Dynamo worker work handler.
panels.title: Worker Request Duration Per Worker (p50 / p90 / p99)
Component Throughput (bytes/sec)
panels.description: Component-level request and response throughput in bytes per second, scoped to the `generate` endpoint. Useful for spotting payload-size regressions or unusually large prompts/responses.
